mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate expanding consumer demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when RSAFS shows consecutive monthly increases exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5%, it signals the end of the zero-lower-bound environment, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive despite FEDFUNDS staying above 0.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline in unemployment and acceleration in nonfarm payrolls indicates a tightening labor market that fuels wage-price spirals, prompting aggressive Fed tightening and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when UNRATE falls below 5.5% and PAYEMS rises above 150,000 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns as earnings constraints lift.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO rises above 2% quarter-over-quarter during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.3 million annualized units, it signals a sharp cooling in the housing sector and broader construction demand, reducing corporate revenues and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive when HOUST remains below 1300 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating real GDP growth and rising CPI indicates demand-pull inflation outpacing supply, which compresses real margins and forces monetary tightening, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annualized and CPI rises above 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the unemployment rate reflects strengthening labor market conditions, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During a stagflation regime, rising CPI combined with elevated federal funds rates erodes real yields and equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise without earnings support.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive when CPI rises above 0.5% month-over-month and FEDFUNDS stays above 0.25% for two consecutive months.
